name: TeamPCP Cascading Attack (LiteLLM + Telnyx)
description: Cascading supply chain attack by TeamPCP group. Compromised Trivy security scanner used to steal LiteLLM's PyPI token. Three-stage malware: credential harvesting, Kubernetes lateral movement, persistent systemd backdoor. LiteLLM has ~95M monthly downloads.

affected_packages.name: litellm
affected_packages.note: Published March 24, 2026. Malicious .pth file auto-executes on Python start. Quarantined ~3 hours later.

affected_packages.name: telnyx
affected_packages.note: Published March 27, 2026. Same RSA-4096 key as litellm compromise.

remediation: Upgrade litellm to >=1.82.9 or downgrade to 1.82.6
Upgrade telnyx to >=4.87.3 or downgrade to 4.87.0
Rotate ALL cloud credentials (AWS, GCP, Azure), SSH keys, database passwords
Audit Kubernetes clusters for unauthorized privileged pods
Check for unknown systemd services on Linux
